title: Hunter's chicken
subtitle: Bacon-wrapped chicken breast with BBQ sauce and melted cheddar
excerpt: Chicken breasts wrapped in smoked bacon, oven-baked until the bacon is crisp, topped with smoky BBQ sauce and melted cheddar. A British pub menu staple rather than a historic dish. Quick, crowd-pleasing, and straightforward, the BBQ sauce can be homemade or good-quality bought.
sourceNotes: Hunter's chicken is a modern British pub dish with no historical lineage. It appeared on pub menus in the 1990s and became standard. It bears no relation to the Italian pollo alla cacciatora or French poulet chasseur. The name is decorative. The dish is notable because it is popular and reproducible at home, which accounts for its widespread recognition.

content.text: The chicken breast must reach 75°C at its thickest point. The bacon wrapping protects the breast from drying out during the oven time. Use streaky bacon rather than back. It wraps more easily and the fat renders properly.

Heat the oven to 200°C. Season the {{chicken-breast}} with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Wrap each breast with 3 to 4 rashers of {{streaky-bacon}}, overlapping to cover fully. Place in a roasting tin. Bake for 25 minutes until the bacon is browned and the chicken is almost cooked through. Spoon the {{bbq-sauce}} generously over each breast. Scatter the grated {{cheddar}} on top. Return to the oven for 8 to 10 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbling and the chicken reads 75°C in the thickest part. Rest for 5 minutes.

items.fix: Check the temperature with a thermometer at 25 minutes. Thin breasts cook faster than thick ones, remove at 70°C and let carry-over bring it to 75°C
items.cause: Overcooked, or chicken breast too thin

items.fix: Streaky bacon needs contact with the hot tin or open air to render. Ensure the wrapped chicken is in a hot oven with the bacon exposed on top
items.cause: Oven temperature too low, or bacon overlapping too thickly
items.symptom: Bacon still pale and flabby rather than browned

content.text: Hunter's chicken appears on the menu of almost every British pub chain and is one of the most ordered pub mains in the country. The domestic version uses the same structure: bacon, BBQ sauce, cheese. It is quick enough for a weeknight and popular enough to appear without apology at a table of adults or children.
